title: Zhang (surname)

infobox.value: [IPA](/wiki/International_Phonetic_Alphabet "International Phonetic Alphabet"): /tʂɑŋ˥/ (Mandarin IPA) Zhāng (Mandarin [Pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin")) Zoeng1 (Cantonese [Jyutping](/wiki/Jyutping "Jyutping"))  Tiuⁿ (Hokkien [Pe̍h-ōe-jī](/wiki/Pe%CC%8Dh-%C5%8De-j%C4%AB "Pe̍h-ōe-jī"))
drawing a bow,[[1]](#cite_note-BaxSag-1) [archer](/wiki/Archery "Archery"), [bowyer](/wiki/Bowyer "Bowyer"), a [classifier](/wiki/Measure_word "Measure word") for flat objects

([[ʈʂáŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Mandarin "Help:IPA/Mandarin") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Zh-zh%C4%81ng.ogg "File:Zh-zhāng.ogg"); [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): [張](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BC%B5 "wiktionary:張"); [simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): [张](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BC%A0 "wikt:张")) is the [third most common surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_surnames "List of common Chinese surnames") in [China](/wiki/China "China") and [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan") (commonly spelled as **Chang** in Taiwan), and it is one of the most common surnames in the world.[[2]](#cite_note-stats-2)[[3]](#cite_note-xinhua-3) It is spoken in the [first tone](/wiki/Chinese_tones "Chinese tones") *Zhāng*. In the [Wade–Giles](/wiki/Wade%E2%80%93Giles "Wade–Giles") system of [romanization](/wiki/Romanization_of_Chinese "Romanization of Chinese"), it is romanized as **Chang**, which is commonly used in [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"). **Cheung** is commonly used in [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong") as a romanization. It is the 24th name on the *[Hundred Family Surnames](/wiki/Hundred_Family_Surnames "Hundred Family Surnames")* poem, contained in the verse 何呂施張 *(Hé Lǚ Shī Zhāng)*.

(*Zhāng*), which is the 40th name on the *[Hundred Family Surnames](/wiki/Hundred_Family_Surnames "Hundred Family Surnames")* poem, and **[仉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BB%89 "wikt:仉")** (*Zhǎng*).

* As the [Hán Tự](/wiki/H%C3%A1n_T%E1%BB%B1 "Hán Tự") for the [Vietnamese surname](/wiki/Vietnamese_surname "Vietnamese surname") **[Trương](/wiki/Truong_(surname) "Truong (surname)")** or **[Trang](/wiki/Trang_(surname) "Trang (surname)")**

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Chinese surname

For the less common surname 章, see [Zhang (surname 章)](/wiki/Zhang_(surname_%E7%AB%A0) "Zhang (surname 章)").

Zhang / Chang

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Zhang surname in [regular script](/wiki/Regular_script "Regular script") | |
| Pronunciation | [IPA](/wiki/International_Phonetic_Alphabet "International Phonetic Alphabet"): /tʂɑŋ˥/ (Mandarin IPA) Zhāng (Mandarin [Pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin")) Zoeng1 (Cantonese [Jyutping](/wiki/Jyutping "Jyutping"))  Tiuⁿ (Hokkien [Pe̍h-ōe-jī](/wiki/Pe%CC%8Dh-%C5%8De-j%C4%AB "Pe̍h-ōe-jī")) |
| Language | Chinese, Korean, Vietnamese |
| Origin | |
| Language | [Old Chinese](/wiki/Old_Chinese "Old Chinese") |
| Derivation | [Nie](/wiki/Nie_(surname) "Nie (surname)") |
| Meaning | drawing a bow,[[1]](#cite_note-BaxSag-1) [archer](/wiki/Archery "Archery"), [bowyer](/wiki/Bowyer "Bowyer"), a [classifier](/wiki/Measure_word "Measure word") for flat objects |

**Zhang** ([[ʈʂáŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Mandarin "Help:IPA/Mandarin") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Zh-zh%C4%81ng.ogg "File:Zh-zhāng.ogg"); [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): [張](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BC%B5 "wiktionary:張"); [simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): [张](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BC%A0 "wikt:张")) is the [third most common surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_surnames "List of common Chinese surnames") in [China](/wiki/China "China") and [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan") (commonly spelled as **Chang** in Taiwan), and it is one of the most common surnames in the world.[[2]](#cite_note-stats-2)[[3]](#cite_note-xinhua-3) It is spoken in the [first tone](/wiki/Chinese_tones "Chinese tones") *Zhāng*. In the [Wade–Giles](/wiki/Wade%E2%80%93Giles "Wade–Giles") system of [romanization](/wiki/Romanization_of_Chinese "Romanization of Chinese"), it is romanized as **Chang**, which is commonly used in [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"). **Cheung** is commonly used in [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong") as a romanization. It is the 24th name on the *[Hundred Family Surnames](/wiki/Hundred_Family_Surnames "Hundred Family Surnames")* poem, contained in the verse 何呂施張 *(Hé Lǚ Shī Zhāng)*.

Zhang is also the pinyin romanization of the less-common surnames **[章](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%AB%A0 "wikt:章")** (*Zhāng*), which is the 40th name on the *[Hundred Family Surnames](/wiki/Hundred_Family_Surnames "Hundred Family Surnames")* poem, and **[仉](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E4%BB%89 "wikt:仉")** (*Zhǎng*).

Today, it is one of the most common surnames in the world at over 100 million people worldwide.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Zhang was listed by the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China "People's Republic of China")'s [National Citizen ID Information System](/wiki/National_Citizen_ID_Information_System "National Citizen ID Information System") as the [third-most-common surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_surnames "List of common Chinese surnames") in [mainland China](/wiki/Mainland_China "Mainland China") (April 2007), with 87.50 million bearers.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

A commonly cited but erroneous [factoid](/wiki/Factoid "Factoid") in the 1990 [Guinness Book of Records](/wiki/Guinness_Book_of_Records "Guinness Book of Records") listed it as the world's most common surname,[[7]](#cite_note-7) but no comprehensive information from China was available at the time and more recent editions have not repeated the claim.

## Transliterations and derivatives

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zhang_(surname)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Transliterations and derivatives")]

| Transliteration | Regions |
| --- | --- |
| Zhang/Chang | China, Taiwan, Korea |
| [Cheung](/wiki/Cheung "Cheung")/Zoeng | [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong"), [Macau](/wiki/Macau "Macau") |
| Cheong | [Macau](/wiki/Macau "Macau"), Malaysia, [Hakka](/wiki/Hakka_Chinese "Hakka Chinese"), [Gan](/wiki/Gan_Chinese "Gan Chinese") |
| [Chong](/wiki/Chong_(surname) "Chong (surname)") | [Hakka](/wiki/Hakka_Chinese "Hakka Chinese") |
| Diong | [Eastern Min](/wiki/Eastern_Min "Eastern Min") |
| Tiong | [Eastern Min](/wiki/Eastern_Min "Eastern Min"), [Philippines](/wiki/Chinese_Filipino "Chinese Filipino") |
| Tsan/Tsaon | [Wu](/wiki/Wu_Chinese "Wu Chinese") ([Shanghainese](/wiki/Shanghainese_language "Shanghainese language")) |
| [Teoh](/wiki/Teoh "Teoh"), [Teo](/wiki/Teo_(surname) "Teo (surname)") | [Hokkien](/wiki/Hokkien "Hokkien"), [Teochew](/wiki/Teochew_Min "Teochew Min") |
| Tew | [Teochew](/wiki/Teochew_Min "Teochew Min") |
| Tjang/Tjiong/Tjon/Tjong | Indonesia |
| [Trương](/wiki/Truong_(surname) "Truong (surname)")/[Trang](/wiki/Trang_(surname) "Trang (surname)") | Vietnam |
| [Jang](/wiki/Jang_(Korean_surname) "Jang (Korean surname)")/[Chang](/wiki/Chang_(Korean_name) "Chang (Korean name)") (장) | Korea |

**Zhang** in [Mandarin](/wiki/Mandarin_Chinese "Mandarin Chinese"), alternatively romanized as **[Chang](/wiki/Chang_(surname) "Chang (surname)")** in [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan") and among the [Chinese diaspora](/wiki/Chinese_diaspora "Chinese diaspora") using older romanization systems. However, Zhang has been the official first-recommended translation for 張 in [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan") since 2017.

### Derivatives

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zhang_(surname)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Derivatives")]

* As the [Hanja](/wiki/Hanja "Hanja") of the [Korean surname](/wiki/Korean_surname "Korean surname") romanized as **[Jang](/wiki/Jang_(Korean_surname) "Jang (Korean surname)")** or **[Chang](/wiki/Chang_(Korean_name) "Chang (Korean name)")** ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 장; [Hanja](/wiki/Hanja "Hanja"): 張)
* As the [Kanji](/wiki/Kanji "Kanji") for the [Japanese surname](/wiki/Japanese_surname "Japanese surname") romanized as **Chō**
* As the [Hán Tự](/wiki/H%C3%A1n_T%E1%BB%B1 "Hán Tự") for the [Vietnamese surname](/wiki/Vietnamese_surname "Vietnamese surname") **[Trương](/wiki/Truong_(surname) "Truong (surname)")** or **[Trang](/wiki/Trang_(surname) "Trang (surname)")**
* Derived as **Canggih, Candra, Hidayat, Irawan, Jaya, Prasetya, Sutiono**, or other [Indonesianized surnames](/wiki/Chinese_Indonesian_surname "Chinese Indonesian surname") among [Chinese Indonesians](/wiki/Chinese_Indonesians "Chinese Indonesians")

## Distribution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zhang_(surname)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Distribution")]

* [Zhang Fei](/wiki/Zhang_Fei "Zhang Fei") (died July or August 221 AD)'s subordinates Fan Qiang (范彊) and Zhang Da (張達) assassinated their commander, decapitated the corpse, and brought the head along with them as they defected to Sun Quan's side.
* [Zhang Gaoli](/wiki/Zhang_Gaoli "Zhang Gaoli") ([Disappearance of Peng Shuai](/wiki/Disappearance_of_Peng_Shuai "Disappearance of Peng Shuai"))
* [Trương Mỹ Lan](/wiki/Tr%C6%B0%C6%A1ng_M%E1%BB%B9_Lan "Trương Mỹ Lan") (Chinese: 張美蘭, born 13 October 1956) On 11 April 2024, Lan was sentenced to death.

For list of people with surname Zhang, see [List of people with surname Zhang](/wiki/List_of_people_with_surname_Zhang "List of people with surname Zhang").

As mentioned above, 張 is the [third-most-common surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_surnames#People's_Republic "List of common Chinese surnames") in [mainland China](/wiki/Mainland_China "Mainland China"), making up 6.83% of the population of the People's Republic of China.[[8]](#cite_note-Eastday-8) In 2019 it was the most common surname in exactly one provincial-level division, [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai") municipality.[[9]](#cite_note-新京报_-_好新闻，无止境-9) In Taiwan, 張 is the [fourth-most-common surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_surnames#Taiwan "List of common Chinese surnames"), making up 5.26% of the population of the Republic of China. In 2019 it was again the third most common surname in Mainland China.[[9]](#cite_note-新京报_-_好新闻，无止境-9)

[Zhang Wei](/wiki/Zhang_Wei_(disambiguation) "Zhang Wei (disambiguation)") (张伟) has been the most common family name and given name combination in China for many years.

Among the Chinese diaspora, the name remains common but takes on various romanizations. "Teo" and "Chong" are amongst the [most common surnames](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_Singaporean_surnames "List of common Chinese Singaporean surnames") among [Chinese Singaporeans](/wiki/Chinese_Singaporeans "Chinese Singaporeans"), listed at 11th and 19th respectively;[[10]](#cite_note-10) "Chang" is the [6th-most-common surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_American_surnames "List of common Chinese American surnames") among [Chinese Americans](/wiki/Chinese_Americans "Chinese Americans"); and "Zhang" was the [7th-most-common particularly Chinese surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_Canadian_surnames "List of common Chinese Canadian surnames") found in a 2010 survey of [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario")'s [Registered Persons Database](/w/index.php?title=Registered_Persons_Database&action=edit&redlink=1 "Registered Persons Database (page does not exist)") of [Canadian health card](/wiki/Canadian_health_card "Canadian health card") recipients.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zhang_(surname)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: History")]

### Characters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zhang_(surname)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Characters")]

張 combines the [Chinese characters](/wiki/Chinese_characters "Chinese characters") [弓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BC%93 "wikt:弓") (*gōng*, "bow") and [長](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%95%B7 "wikt:長") ([simp.](/wiki/Simplified_characters "Simplified characters") [长](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%95%BF "wikt:长"), *cháng*, "long" or "wide"). It originally meant "to open up" or "to spread" as an arching bow, but as a common noun in modern use it is a [measure word](/wiki/Measure_word "Measure word") for flat objects such as paper and cloth, like the English "sheet of".

### Families

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zhang_(surname)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Families")]

The traditional origin of the surname 張 ([Old Chinese](/wiki/Old_Chinese "Old Chinese"): *\*C. traŋ*[[1]](#cite_note-BaxSag-1)) is rooted in Chinese legend. The fifth son of the [Yellow Emperor](/wiki/Yellow_Emperor "Yellow Emperor"), [Qing Yangshi](/w/index.php?title=Qing_Yangshi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Qing Yangshi (page does not exist)") ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): [青](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9D%92 "wikt:青")[阳](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%98%B3 "wikt:阳")[氏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B0%8F "wikt:氏"); [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): [青](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%9D%92 "wikt:青")[陽](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%99%BD "wikt:陽")[氏](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%B0%8F "wikt:氏"); [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Qīng Yángshì*), had a son [Hui](/w/index.php?title=Hui_(son_of_Qing_Yangshi)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hui (son of Qing Yangshi) (page does not exist)") ([挥](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%8C%A5 "wikt:挥"); [揮](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%8F%AE "wikt:揮"); *Huī*) who was inspired by the Heavenly Bow [constellation](/wiki/Chinese_constellation "Chinese constellation") ([天弓星](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%BC%A7%E7%9F%A2 "zh:弧矢"), *Tiān Gōng Xīng*) to invent the bow and arrow. Hui was then promoted to "First Bow" ([弓](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%BC%93 "wikt:弓")[正](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E6%AD%A3 "wikt:正"), *Gōng Zhèng*) and bestowed the surname 張, which – when broken into its constituent [radicals](/wiki/Radical_(Chinese_character) "Radical (Chinese character)") – means "widening bow" or "archer". Its [Middle Chinese](/wiki/Middle_Chinese_language "Middle Chinese language") pronunciation has been reconstructed as *Trjang*.[[1]](#cite_note-BaxSag-1)

### Other origins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Zhang_(surname)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Other origins")]

* for some families, it is traced back to Xie Zhang (解張), whose style name was Zhang Hou (張侯, lit “Marquis Zhang”) a noble in [Jin](/wiki/Jin_(Chinese_state) "Jin (Chinese state)") during the [Spring and Autumn period](/wiki/Spring_and_Autumn_period "Spring and Autumn period").
* from the family of [Zhang Liao](/wiki/Zhang_Liao "Zhang Liao") (張遼), an official in [Cao Wei](/wiki/Cao_Wei "Cao Wei") during [Three Kingdoms](/wiki/Three_Kingdoms "Three Kingdoms") period. Zhāng Liao's family had changed from *[Nie](/wiki/Nie_(surname) "Nie (surname)")* to *Zhang* to avoid association with his disgraced ancestor [Nie Yi](/w/index.php?title=Nie_Yi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nie Yi (page does not exist)") (聶壹).[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* the surname is also traced back to Long Youna, chief of a minority ethnic groups during the Three Kingdoms period, who was given the Chinese surname Zhang (張) by [Zhuge Liang](/wiki/Zhuge_Liang "Zhuge Liang"), the prime minister of [Shu](/wiki/Shu_Han "Shu Han").

Surname list
